criteria.evidence: Reverse-map table quotes 12 JD lines verbatim ('Run a high-velocity experimentation program: design experiments, define guardrail metrics, and make ship/no-ship calls from results.') each paired with specific evidence ('Owns experimentation roadmap; made the ship call on onboarding redesign; killed mandatory tutorial via holdout (-6% activation)').
criteria.reason: Comprehensive coverage of every required and nice-to-have JD line, each quoting the JD and citing concrete persona evidence. No major JD line dropped.

criteria.evidence: Experimentation fundamentals=strong, analytics/SQL=strong, measurable funnel impact=strong, experimentation program=strong; 'own a growth area: roadmap, prioritize backlog, write PRDs'=partial; nice-to-have 'full product roadmap and backlog end to end'=GAP.
criteria.reason: Every label matches the expectations answer key: all four experimentation/data lines correctly strong (no underselling), roadmap/PRD line correctly partial, full-roadmap nice-to-have correctly GAP (expected partial/GAP). No mislabel of the anti-trap line.

criteria.evidence: 'I ran ~60 experiments/year ... -> the textbook approach is a fixed-horizon A/B test sized by MDE -> I leaned on CUPED and sequential testing to get reads faster ... accepting the added analytical complexity for velocity.'
criteria.reason: Each of five strong lines has a concrete what-you-did -> SOTA -> trade-off meta-move, not a restated bullet. The CUPED/sequential-vs-fixed-horizon and vanity-metric examples are specific and defensible.

criteria.evidence: GAP map lists full-roadmap+backlog as priority 🔴, PRD craft 🔴, quarterly planning 🟠, prioritization framework 🟠, product-sense 🟠, design/technical depth 🟡, plus a ⚠️-flagged per-feature-numbers gap.
criteria.reason: The must-find gaps (full product ownership, deeper design/technical partnership) are present, mapped to JD lines, and prioritized with a sensible weight×weakness÷cost feel. Dock one point: expectations call these gaps moderate (🟠) given the role suits him; the artifact escalates the two core ones to 🔴, slightly over-weighting the stretch.

criteria.evidence: 'This is a resurfacing-plus-stretch profile, not a learning-from-scratch one ... The genuine stretch is the product-craft half: owning a full product roadmap and backlog across a quarter, writing full PRDs ... lean on the fact that the role is experimentation-heavy, so they're strongest exactly where this role is weighted most.'
criteria.reason: Matches the expected verdict precisely: sharpening the experimentation strength while growing into full product craft. Names the partial honestly without underselling the experimentation edge; no rubber-stamping of the product-ownership gap.

highest_leverage_fix: None required to pass. Minor: the FIT step prompt could anchor GAP-priority color to the overall fit proportion (when the role plays to a strength, a stretch gap is typically 🟠 not 🔴) to avoid over-escalating moderate gaps.
